instructions.en: Choose the correct option for each question. Each correct answer carries 1 mark. Each incorrect answer deducts 0.25 mark.

questions.en: In this sentence from "A Letter to God", which literary device is used by the author to describe the field? "The field was white as if covered with salt."
According to the author of "Coorg", the people of Coorg are possibly descendants of the __________ or the __________.
"A plague of locusts would have left more than this." Lencho made this remark as his crops were destroyed by:
In the story "A Letter to God", what was the most likely response that the postmaster expected in Lencho's second letter to God?
The ceremonies taking place in the sandstone amphitheatre of Pretoria refer to the celebration of South Africa's first __________ and __________ Government.
With reference to the chapter "Tea from Assam", what is the speaker's tone in the quoted sentence: "You seem to have done your homework before coming"?

With reference to "Madam Rides the Bus", read the statements and choose the correct option. Statement I: Valli had many playmates of her age in her neighbourhood. Statement II: Valli gained many new unusual experiences by watching the street.

options.en: Statement I is True and Statement II is False.
Statement I is False and Statement II is True.
Both Statement I and Statement II are True.
Both Statement I and Statement II are False.

questions.en: In "Tea from Assam", Rajvir says that no one really knows who discovered tea but there are many legends. He suggests that the traditional stories regarding the origin of tea are not __________ till today.

"The structure they created formed the basis of one of the harshest, most inhumane societies that the world has ever known." What does "structure" refer to in this sentence from "Long Walk to Freedom"?
"Coorgi homes have a tradition of hospitality and they are more than willing to recount numerous tales of valour...." Which option is closest to the meaning of "to recount"?
"Man's goodness is a flame that can be hidden but never extinguished." Choose a word or phrase with the same meaning as "extinguished".
On receiving the letter from the postmaster, Lencho did not seem surprised because:

options.en: He was confident of receiving a reply from God.
He had dreamt about God's reply.
He knew the postmaster was a good man.

questions.en: Why does even the most laidback individual become adventurous in Coorg?

options.en: Because Coorgi people do not welcome laidback persons.
Because Coorgi culture is adventure friendly.
Because the environment offers opportunities for adventure sports and activities.
Because laidback people find it difficult to while away time at Coorg.

questions.en: "Hey! A tea garden!" Rajvir cried excitedly. But Pranjol did not share Rajvir's excitement. Why?

options.en: Because Pranjol did not like tea gardens.
Because Pranjol was familiar with the landscape dotted with tea gardens.
Because Pranjol was annoyed with Rajvir.

questions.en: "Thirty-one minutes," Ausable said moodily. "The appointment was for twelve thirty. I wish I knew how you learned about the report, Max." What report was referred to by Ausable? [The Midnight Visitor]

options.en: A report concerning some new missiles

questions.en: Horace Danby never got a chance to work on a new plan after the Shotover Grange episode. Why? [A Question of Trust]

options.en: He was exhausted of all plans.
He went away to a different place.
He was upset with his accomplice.

questions.en: Fowler felt let down. Why? [The Midnight Visitor]

options.en: Fowler did not get good food to eat.
Fowler realised that the secret agent was not coming to the hotel.
The secret agent led him to a hotel room which was rather gloomy.
Fowler could not understand what the secret agent said.

questions.en: How did the junior assistant of the law firm feel when he was sent to serve summons? [The Hack Driver]
"Brilliant scientist though he was, Griffin was rather a lawless person." Which option expresses the writer's attitude to Griffin? [Footprints without Feet]
"Shivering with cold he hurried to Drury Lane...." Why did Griffin visit Drury Lane? [Footprints without Feet]

options.en: He wanted to watch a theatre.
He wanted to spend some time at a shop.
He wanted to get some food and clothing.

questions.en: With reference to "The Ball Poem", choose the statement that correctly identifies the underlined word in: "I would not intrude on him."

options.en: The poet would not intrude on the boy.
The passerby would not intrude on the boy.
The boy would not intrude on the poet.
The speaker would not intrude on the poet.

questions.en: What does the boy feel when he loses the ball, as mentioned in "The Ball Poem"?
In the poem "Animals", the poet claims that he can live with:
The small boy in "The Ball Poem" is experiencing:
"There is a languid, emerald sea, where the sole inhabitant is me...." [Amanda] Choose the correct synonym for "languid".
In the poem "Amanda", what is Amanda's primary desire?
According to the poet Leslie Norris, where should the tiger ideally be? [A Tiger in the Zoo]

options.en: Quietly walking in a cage in the zoo.
Sleeping in a concrete cell in the zoo.
Snarling around the houses at the jungle's edge.
Resting under the sun outside the cage.

questions.en: Read the statements with reference to "A Tiger in the Zoo". Statement 1: Ferocious animals like tigers are a threat to the natural and human world. Statement 2: Zoo animals become quite placid inside their cage.

options.en: Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are true.
Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are false.
Statement 1 is true and Statement 2 is false.
Statement 1 is false and Statement 2 is true.

questions.en: In "The Tale of Custard the Dragon", how is Custard portrayed before the arrival of the pirate?

options.en: As a cowardly and timid dragon.
As a fierce and intimidating dragon.
As a clumsy and lazy dragon.
As an intelligent and cunning dragon.

questions.en: Identify the literary device used in this line from "Animals": "I think I could turn and live with animals...."
Identify the correct question tag to fill in the blank: He is a teacher, __________
Select the correct combination of these sentences: He has no strength. He has no intelligence.

options.en: He has neither strength and no either intelligence.
He has no strength and either no intelligence.
He has neither strength nor intelligence.
He has neither strength and intelligence.

questions.en: Choose the correct option to fill in the blank: All tickets for the cultural night have been __________.
Did you _________ the references from Shakespeare's drama? Fill in the blank.
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence: She gave __________ money she had to the beggar.
Choose the option that correctly changes the voice of the sentence: I cooked all the dessert items served yesterday.

options.en: All the dessert items served yesterday were cooked by me.
All the dessert items served yesterday were my cooking.
All the dessert items are cooked by me which were served yesterday.
All the dessert items cooked by me was served yesterday.

questions.en: Choose the indirect form of the sentence: My friend said to me, "Have you had your lunch?"

options.en: My friend asked me if I had had my lunch.
My friend told me if I had my lunch.
My friend said to me if I have had my lunch.
My friend asked me if I had my lunch.

questions.en: Choose the correct tense form: The train __________ before we reached the station.
Fill in the blank: Lina is _________ careful than her sister.
Choose the correct options to fill in the blanks: __________ the long run, it is important to listen __________ our instincts before taking a major decision.

Read the passage and answer: "The house - the only one in the entire valley - sat on the crest of a low hill. From this height one could see the river and the field of ripe corn dotted with the flowers that always promised a good harvest. The only thing the earth needed was a downpour or at least a shower. Throughout the morning Lencho - who knew his fields intimately - had done nothing else but see the sky towards the north-east." What did Lencho do throughout the morning?

Read the passage and answer: "The house - the only one in the entire valley - sat on the crest of a low hill. From this height one could see the river and the field of ripe corn dotted with the flowers that always promised a good harvest. The only thing the earth needed was a downpour or at least a shower. Throughout the morning Lencho - who knew his fields intimately - had done nothing else but see the sky towards the north-east." Choose the correct antonym for the word "crest".

Read the passage and answer: "Rabindranath Tagore was born in 1861, into one of the foremost families of Bengal. He was the fourteenth child of Debendranath Tagore, who headed the Brahmo Samaj. The family house at Jorasanko in Calcutta was a hive of cultural and intellectual activity. Tagore was educated by private tutors and he first visited Europe in 1878. He started writing at an early age, and his talent was recognised by Bankimchandra Chatterjee, the leading writer of the day. In the 1890s Tagore lived mainly in rural East Bengal, managing family estates. In the early 1900s he was involved in the Swadeshi campaign against the British but he withdrew from it when the movement turned violent. In 1912 he came to England with Gitanjali, an English translation of some of his religious lyrics. It was acclaimed by W.B. Yeats and later published by Macmillan, leading directly to his winning the Nobel Prize for Literature in 1913." Who was Rabindranath Tagore's contemporary writer in Bengal?

Based on the passage about Rabindranath Tagore, arrange the events chronologically: (i) He was awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature. (ii) He was born as the fourteenth child of Debendranath Tagore. (iii) He came to England. (iv) He first visited Europe in 1878.
Based on the passage about Rabindranath Tagore, why did he withdraw from the Swadeshi movement?

options.en: Because it hampered his writing activities.
Because he was not a military man.
Because he wanted to set up a university.
